title: ## STANDARD FOR QUICK-FROZEN PEACHES CXS 75-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2019, 2022.

sections.section: ## STANDARD FOR QUICK-FROZEN PEACHES CXS 75-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Document title and amendment note: heading for the 'Standard for Quick-Frozen Peaches (CXS 75-1981)' noting adoption date and amendments in 2019 and 2022, with amendments made to Section 6.4 on labelling of non-retail containers.

Scope: this standard applies to quick-frozen peaches (Prunus persica L., excluding nectarines) offered for direct consumption without further processing except repacking, and does not apply to products intended for further processing or other industrial uses.

Product and process description: quick-frozen peaches are made from fresh, sound, properly ripened Prunus persica (excluding nectarines) packed with or without dry sugar or syrup and quick-frozen so the maximum crystallization range is passed rapidly and product center temperature reaches −18°C; handling must maintain quality (referencing the Code of Practice CXC 8-1976), and presentations must declare varietal type (freestone/clingstone), flesh colour (white, yellow, red, green) and style (whole, halves, quarters, sliced, pieces, diced up to 15 mm), with other styles permitted if distinctive, meeting requirements and labelled to avoid consumer confusion.
Defines quick-frozen peaches as product from fresh, sound, properly ripened Prunus persica L. (excluding nectarine varieties), packed with or without dry sugar or syrup and packaged.

Specifies the quick-freezing process: rapid passage through the maximum crystallization range, completion only when product core temperature reaches -18 °C after thermal stabilization, and permits recognized repacking under controlled conditions.

Requires handling to maintain quality during transportation, storage and distribution up to final sale and recommends following the Code of Practice for Processing and Handling of Quick Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).

Describes presentation requirements including varietal type, colour type, prescribed styles (whole, halves, quarters, sliced, pieces, diced with size limit) and allows other distinctive presentations provided they meet the standard and are adequately labelled to avoid misleading consumers.
Requires varietal designation as either freestone (pit separates readily from flesh) or clingstone (pit adheres to flesh).
Defines colour-type designations for ripe flesh—white, yellow, red, and green—by the predominant flesh colour ranges for each varietal type.
Specifies permitted product styles (whole, halves, quarters, sliced, pieces, diced) and defines each style, including diced maximum dimension of 15 mm on one edge.

Permits other presentation styles provided they are sufficiently distinct from listed styles, comply with all standard requirements, and are clearly described on the label to avoid consumer confusion or misleading.

Sets essential composition and quality framework: allows specified sugars as optional ingredients; prescribes soluble solids ranges and syrup limits for composition, defines compositional defectives and lot acceptance (AQL 6.5); and establishes quality factor requirements, visual defect definitions, sample sizes, tolerances and lot acceptance criteria (including pit tolerances) for inspection and grading.
Lists permitted optional sugars: sucrose, invert sugar/syrup, dextrose, fructose, glucose syrup and dried glucose syrup.

Prescribes compositional rules: soluble solids limits for products with dry sugar (18–35% m/m) and with syrup (15–30% m/m), limits syrup usage to that required to cover and fill spaces, defines compositional "defective" (with a 5% m/m allowance) and requires lot acceptance per a sampling plan with AQL 6.5.
For peaches prepared with dry sugars, total soluble solids of liquid from thawed, comminuted sample must be between 18 and 35% m/m, measured by refractometer at 20 °C.

For peaches prepared with syrup, syrup must only cover and fill spaces between fruit and total soluble solids of liquid from a thawed, comminuted sample must be between 15 and 30% m/m, measured by refractometer at 20 °C.
Defines a compositional "defective" as any sample unit outside the soluble solids ranges in Sections 3.2.1–3.2.2 provided it does not exceed those limits by more than 5% m/m soluble solids.
States lot acceptance for composition: a lot is acceptable when the number of compositional defectives does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Establishes quality factors: lists general product requirements (clean, free from foreign flavour/odour, varietally similar, uniform colour, etc.), defines visual defects, prescribes standard sample sizes and detailed tolerances/point-scoring tables by style, and sets lot acceptance criteria using an AQL of 6.5 with a pit-tolerance provision for non-whole styles.

Specifies general quality requirements for quick-frozen peaches including being clean and practically free from foreign material, free from foreign flavour/odour, of similar varietal characteristics and uniform colour, and subject to tolerances for discolouration, blemishes, extraneous vegetable matter, excess trimming/mechanical damage, fibrousness, overripeness/mushiness, peel, whole pits (except in whole style) and pit fragments.

Defines visual defects: discolouration (oxidation/bruising), blemish (insect/scab damage), stalks, extraneous vegetable matter (EVM), excess trim/mechanical damage, fibrous units, overripe/mushy units, and pit fragments (hard/sharp pieces at least 3 mm in any dimension).

Specifies standard sample sizes for defect assessment: whole style 20 whole fruits, halved/quartered 30 halves/quarters, and sliced/diced/pieces 300 g of drained fruit (refer to relevant Codex sampling methods).

Provides detailed tolerances for visual defects by style via tables that assign minor/major/serious defect point values and total allowable points (e.g., totals by category and combined), including style-specific measurement units and a whole-pit tolerance of 1 per 3 kg, to be applied in sampling and lot grading.

Defines a sample unit as "defective" for quality factors when taken under an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5 and adjusted to a standard sample unit size for applying visual defect tolerances, and (as given) includes any sample unit that fails to meet the general requirements of Section 3.3.1.

States that a lot is acceptable when the number of "defectives" (per 3.3.5) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5, and except for the "whole" style the number of whole pits must not exceed the tolerance on a sample average basis.
Lists food additives and maximum levels for the product, specifying citric acid is permitted but limited by Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP).

Hygiene provisions require product preparation and handling per the General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant Codex codes, that the product be free from objectionable matter to the extent possible under GMP, and when tested be free from microorganisms, parasites or microbial substances in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.
Heading with substantive labelling provisions to follow for quick-frozen peaches that supplement the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1/119-85).

Specifies detailed labelling requirements for the product name: the label must include the word "peaches" and, in conjunction or close proximity, the style, packing medium, varietal designation, a quick-frozen/frozen indication, and declaration of flesh colour by illustration or nomenclature.

sections.section: 6.1.1 The name of the product as declared on the label shall include the designation "peaches".
sections.summary: Requires that the product name declared on the label include the designation "peaches".

sections.section: 6.1.2 In addition, there shall appear on the label in conjunction with or in close proximity to the word "peaches":
sections.summary: Requires that the label show, in conjunction or close proximity to "peaches", the style (e.g., "whole", "halves", "slices", "diced") and the packing medium (naming the sweetener and whether as such or as syrup).

sections.section: 6.1.3 If the product is produced in accordance with subsection 2.4.4, the label shall contain in close proximity to the word "peaches" such additional words or phrases that will avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.
sections.summary: States that if the product is produced under subsection 2.4.4, the label must include additional words or phrases near "peaches" to avoid misleading or confusing consumers.

sections.section: 6.1.4 Peaches of distinct varietal types shall be designated: "freestone" or "clingstone", as appropriate (see subsection 2.4.1).
sections.summary: Requires peaches of distinct varietal types to be designated on the label as either "freestone" or "clingstone" as appropriate.

sections.section: 6.1.5 In addition, there shall appear on the label the words "quick-frozen" except that the term "frozen" i  may be applied in  countries  where  this  term  is  customarily  used  for  describing  the  product  processed  in  accordance  with subsection 2.2 of this standard.
sections.summary: Requires the words "quick-frozen" on the label, except that the term "frozen" may be used in countries where it is customarily applied to products processed according to subsection 2.2 of the standard.

sections.section: 6.1.6 The colour type of the flesh of the peaches shall be declared either by illustration or by nomenclature.
sections.summary: Requires declaration of the flesh colour type of the peaches on the label either by illustration or by nomenclature.
Requires packages to bear clear directions for storage from purchase to use and clear directions for thawing.

For quick-frozen peaches sold in bulk, requires that the mandatory information either appear on the container or in accompanying documents, but mandates the container display the product name with "quickfrozen" (or "frozen" where customary) and the manufacturer/packer name and address.
Advises that labelling of non-retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Requires packaging for quick-frozen peaches to protect organoleptic and quality characteristics, prevent bacteriological and other contamination, guard against dehydration and leakage as far as technologically practicable, and not impart any odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics to the product.

States that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with the standard shall be those contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.
